seoTitle: Thornton vs Thuddungra — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Thornton and Thuddungra. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Thornton and Thuddungra.
heading: Thornton vs Thuddungra — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Thornton, New South Wales and Thuddungra, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Thuddungra has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Thornton (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Thornton or Thuddungra safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Thornton has a flood risk score of 33/100, while Thuddungra scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Thornton scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Thuddungra scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Thornton or Thuddungra?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Thuddungra has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
